design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect all stolen items from mansion while avoiding enemy cats; use doors and environmental hazards (trampolines, microwaves) strategically to stun enemies and progress through rooms
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between speed (time limit pressure with escalating threats) and safety (navigating around faster, more numerous enemies); bonus multiplier system incentivizes consecutive pair collection despite danger
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Mappy, a police mouse detective recovering stolen goods from a cat crime hideout, using wit and environmental mechanics rather than direct combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects combat-focused gameplay in favor of puzzle-like environmental manipulation and timing; emphasizes traversal skill and spatial awareness over reflexes; cats are obstacles to outsmart, not defeat; bonus system rewards risky play patterns (collecting consecutive pairs for multiplier stacking)

difficulty.lives_system: Single life lost when touching unstunned cats while moving, falling off screen without trampoline catch, or touching the Gosenzo coin; game ends when all lives exhausted
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-round checkpoints; failure resets entire round; bonus rounds provide score opportunities but not progression safety nets
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Escalating difficulty is hardcoded (more Meowky cats, increased speed, new hazards like vanishing floors and hanging bells added progressively); time pressure mechanic (hurry-up warning spawning additional cats and Gosenzo coin) punishes cautious play; player can choose safer vs. riskier collection paths but cannot adjust overall challenge

progression.unlock_mechanism: Rounds unlock sequentially; new difficulty modifiers unlock with progression (attic access, bells above trampolines, vanishing floor sections); bonus rounds appear at intervals
replayability_hooks: Score-chasing via consecutive pair multiplier system (starts at 2x, caps at 6x)
Risk-reward gameplay allowing different route choices through mansion
Bonus round balloon-popping challenges for point accumulation
Increasingly complex enemy patterns and environmental hazards per round

world_building_techniques: Character naming establishes thematic universe (Mappy = police mouse, Goro = cat gang leader, Meowky = underling cats)
Visual setting (mansion hideout) implies heist/crime narrative without exposition
Stolen item variety (stereos, TVs, paintings, computers, safes) creates implicit loot-recovery fantasy

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss fights; Goro (gang leader cat) functions as an advanced enemy type rather than boss encounter—larger, red colored, can be stunned by collecting items behind his position for bonus points; Gosenzo coin serves as escalating threat spawned by time pressure rather than true boss
notable_bosses: Goro (large red cat leader, stuns briefly when item collected while hiding behind it)
Gosenzo coin (time-pressure spawn, immune to microwaves, can kill Mappy mid-air)

faction_indicators: Player (Mappy, police mouse) vs. Cat Gang (Goro leader, Meowky underlings)
Law enforcement (recovery of stolen goods) vs. criminal element (thieving cats)
danger_signals: Hurry-up warning indicating time pressure escalation
Increasing cat spawn rate and speed as rounds progress
Gosenzo coin appearance as threat-level indicator
Flashing microwave doors indicate high-risk/high-reward mechanics
environmental_storytelling: Paired stolen item types (stereos, TVs, paintings, computers, safes) suggest organized theft operation
Door mechanisms and trampoline architecture create tactile puzzle environment distinct from typical platformer
